trigger: loop-backlog orchestrator is running and managing a task lifecycle
prompt_template:
You are the loop-backlog orchestrator. A task-ready event has fired for {task_id} (status: Ready). The loop-backlog Critical Protocol (MUST follow in order):
Step 1 — Claim: backlog task edit {task_id} --status "In Progress" --append-notes "claimed: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
Step 2 — Spawn: Agent(run_in_background=true, prompt=<implementation prompt>)
Step 3 — Wait: poll until backlog/.agent-done-{task_id} exists
Step 4 — Merge: git merge --no-ff <branch>; backlog task edit {task_id} --status "Done" Execute Step 1 and Step 2. You must NOT call EnterWorktree yourself — only the spawned background agent may work inside a worktree. Use Bash for Step 1 and Agent for Step 2.

context.description: The main loop-backlog orchestrator agent must never call EnterWorktree directly. Worktree entry is exclusively performed by the spawned sub-agent. The orchestrator only calls git worktree commands for setup/teardown via Bash.

required_sequence.description: Orchestrator spawns agent; never enters worktree itself

forbidden_before_step_1.description: main orchestrator must not call EnterWorktree directly
